segments.text: But now listen, O Jacob My servant, Israel, whom I have chosen.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: This is the word of the LORD, your Maker, who formed you from the womb and who will help you:
Do not be afraid, O Jacob My servant, Jeshurun, [^157] whom I have chosen.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For I will pour water on the thirsty land, and currents on the dry ground. I will pour out My Spirit on your descendants, and My blessing on your offspring.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They will sprout among the grasslike willows[^158] by flowing streams.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: One will say, ‘I belong to the LORD,’another will call himself by the name of Jacob, and still another will write on his hand, ‘The LORD’s,’and will take the name of Israel.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Thus says the LORD, the King and Redeemer of Israel, the LORD of Hosts:
I am the first and I am the last, and there is no God but Me.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Who then is like Me? Let him say so! Let him declare his case before Me, since I established an ancient people. Let him foretell the things to come, and what is to take place.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Do not tremble or fear. Have I not told you and declared it long ago? You are My witnesses! Is there any God but Me? There is no other Rock; I know not one.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: All makers of idols are nothing, and the things they treasure are worthless. Their witnesses fail to see or comprehend, so they are put to shame.
Who fashions a god or casts an idol which profits him nothing?
Behold, all his companions will be put to shame, for the craftsmen themselves are only human. Let them all assemble and take their st and; they will all be brought to terror and shame.

The blacksmith takes a tool and labors over the coals; he fashions an idol with hammers and forges it with his strong arms. Yet he grows hungry and loses his strength; he fails to drink water and grows faint.

The woodworker extends a measuring line; he marks it out with a stylus; he shapes it with chisels and outlines it with a compass. He fashions it in the likeness of man, like man in all his glory,
that it may dwell in a shrine.
He cuts down cedarsor retrieves a cypress[^159] or oak. He lets it grow strong among the trees of the forest. He plants a laurel, and the rain makes it grow.
It serves as fuel for man. He takes some of it to warm himself, and he kindles a fire and bakes his bread; he even fashions it into a god and worships it; he makes an idol and bows down to it.
He burns half of it in the fire, and he roasts meat on that half. He eats the roast and is satisfied. Indeed, he warms himself and says,
Ah! I am warm; I see the fire.
segments.speaker: Indeed, he warms himself and

segments.text: From the rest he makes a god, his graven image. He bows down to it and worships; he prays to it and says,
Save me, for you are my god.
segments.speaker: Indeed, he warms himself and

segments.text:
They do not comprehend or discern, for He has shut their eyes so they cannot see
and closed their minds so they cannot understand.
And no one considers in his heart, no one has the knowledge or insight to say,

I burned half of it in the fire, and I baked bread on its coals; 
I roasted meat and I ate. Shall I make something detestable with the rest of it? Shall I bow down to a block of wood?
segments.speaker: Indeed, he warms himself and

segments.text: He feeds on ashes. His deluded heart has led him astray, and he cannot deliver himself or say,
Is not this thing in my right hand a lie?
segments.speaker: Indeed, he warms himself and

segments.text: Remember these things, O Jacob, for you are My servant, O Israel. I have made you, and you are My servant; O Israel, I will never forget you.

I have blotted out your transgressions like a cloud, and your sins like a mist.
Return to Me, for I have redeemed you.

Sing for joy, O heavens, for the LORD has done this; shout aloud, O depths of the earth. Break forth in song, O mountains, you forests and all your trees. For the LORD has redeemed Jacob, and revealed His glory in Israel.
Thus says the LORD, your Redeemer who formed you from the womb:
I am the LORD, who has made all things, who alone stretched out the heavens, who by Myself spread out the earth,
segments.speaker: Indeed, he warms himself and

segments.text: who foils the signs of false prophets and makes fools of diviners, who confounds the wise and turns their knowledge into nonsense,
segments.speaker: Indeed, he warms himself and

segments.text: who confirms the message of His servant and fulfills the counsel of His messengers, who says of Jerusalem,‘She will be inhabited,’and of the cities of Judah,‘They will be rebuilt, and I will restore their ruins,’
segments.speaker: Indeed, he warms himself and

segments.text: who says to the depths of the sea,‘Be dry, and I will dry up your currents,’
segments.speaker: Indeed, he warms himself and

segments.text: who says of Cyrus,‘My shepherd will fulfill all that I desire,’who says of Jerusalem,‘She will be rebuilt,’and of the temple,‘Let its foundation be laid.’
segments.speaker: Indeed, he warms himself and
